text:
# In the Family Court		Case No: [Case number]

In the Family Court		Case No: [Case number]

sitting at [Court name]

Schedule 1 to the Children Act 1989

The Child Support Act 1991

The [Relationship] / [Family] of [applicant name] and [respondent name]

After hearing [name the advocate(s) who appeared]

After consideration of the documents lodged by the parties

(in the case of an order made without notice) After reading the statements and hearing the witnesses specified in para [para number] of the Recitals below

ORDER MADE BY [NAME OF JUDGE] ON [DATE] SITTING IN [OPEN COURT] / [PRIVATE] [FOLLOWING A [RESERVED] / [WRITTEN] / [EXTEMPORE] JUDGMENT GIVEN ON [DATE]]
(delete as appropriate)

WARNING: IF YOU DO NOT COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER, YOU MAY BE HELD TO BE IN CONTEMPT OF COURT AND YOU MAY BE SENT TO PRISON, BE FINED, OR HAVE YOUR ASSETS SEIZED.

The parties

The applicant is [applicant name]

The [first] respondent is [respondent name]

[The second respondent is [respondent name]]

[The third [etc] respondent is [respondent name]]

[The intervener is [intervener name]]

(specify if any party acts by a litigation friend)

Definitions

Child[ren] of the family

The [“child of the family” is] / [“children of the family” are]:

[child full name] born on [date];

[child full name] born on [date];

[etc].

Family home

The “family home” means [family home address] registered at HM Land Registry with title number [family home title no].

Other properties

“[Other property name]” means [other property address] registered at HM Land Registry with title number [title number];

[etc].

New home

The “new home” means [[full address] registered at HM Land Registry with title number [title number]] / [the property [chosen by the [applicant] / [respondent] [up to the value of £[amount], provided it is a reasonable investment for the [respondent] / applicant]] / [agreed between the parties or in default of agreement determined by the court]].

metadata.source: Standard Family Orders — Volume 1: Financial Remedies (May 2026)
